summary.intent: Improve UX of the browser session management page by showing a 'creating' state while new sessions are being initialized.
summary.outcome: Added a creating/loading state to the new session button in page-browser.js, disabling it and showing progress feedback during session creation. Changes were committed and pushed.

repo: validate_url() previously only allowed http/https schemes; about:blank required a special case to support new session creation.
UI buttons across the app (MCP/Skills pages) use provider-btn, provider-btn-sm, provider-btn-secondary, and provider-btn-danger CSS classes as the standard style.

code.finding: New session button needed a 'creating' signal/state to disable the button and display progress feedback while the session is being initialized.

workflow: JS changes are linted before committing.
Work is done in a git worktree at /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/plan-browser-viewing-HOHno.

friction: No visible feedback during session creation caused user uncertainty about whether the action was working.
